2345	// TestExecuteBeadStatusMapping exercises the real (agent, orchestrator, Land)
2346	// stack through the script harness for every status the classifier produces.
2347	// Each subtest sets up an isolated real git repo, drives the script harness
2348	// with a per-attempt directive file that encodes the scenario (no commits,
2349	// dirty worktree, failing exit, conflicting sibling), and asserts that the
2350	// Status written onto the ExecuteBeadResult by ApplyLandingToResult matches
2351	// the expected supervisor-visible status.
2352	//
2353	// Migrated off fakeExecuteBeadGit / fakeAgentRunner per concerns.md §testing
2354	// ("no mocks, period"; "never mock the thing you are testing"). Parent bead
2355	// ddx-d9df348d.
2356	func TestExecuteBeadStatusMapping(t *testing.T) {
2357		// setupRepo builds a real git repo with one seed commit, then commits a
2358		// single open bead on top so the execute-bead command can find it.
2359		// Returns (workDir, baseSHA, runGit).
2360		setupRepo := func(t *testing.T) (string, string, func(args ...string) string) {
2361			t.Helper()
2362			workDir := t.TempDir()
2363	
2364			scrubEnv := func() []string {
2365				parent := os.Environ()
2366				env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
2367				for _, kv := range parent {
2368					if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
2369						continue
2370					}
2371					env = append(env, kv)
2372				}
2373				return env
2374			}
2375			runGit := func(args ...string) string {
2376				t.Helper()
2377				cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
2378				cmd.Dir = workDir
2379				cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
2380				out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
2381				require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
2382				return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
2383			}
2384	
2385			runGit("init", "-b", "main")
2386			runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
2387			runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
2388			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
2389			runGit("add", ".")
2390			runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
2391	
2392			seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
2393				ID:        "status-bead",
2394				Title:     "Test status mapping",
2395				Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
2396				IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
2397			})
2398			runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
2399			runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
2400			baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
2401			return workDir, baseSHA, runGit
2402		}
2403	
2404		// runWithDirective writes a directive file, invokes execute-bead with the
2405		// real agent runner and the script harness, and returns the parsed result.
2406		// No fake git or runner — the full ExecuteBead → LandBeadResult → Land
2407		// pipeline runs against the real repo.
2408		runWithDirective := func(t *testing.T, workDir, baseSHA, directives string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
2409			t.Helper()
2410			directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
2411			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(directives), 0o644))
2412			f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
2413			f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
2414			return runExecuteBead(t, f, nil, "status-bead",
2415				"--from", baseSHA,
2416				"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
2417		}
2418	
2419		t.Run("merged success", func(t *testing.T) {
2420			workDir, baseSHA, _ := setupRepo(t)
2421			// Real commit + clean fast-forward → outcome "merged" → status success.
2422			res := runWithDirective(t, workDir, baseSHA,
2423				"create-file out.txt content\ncommit feat: add out\n")
2424			assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
2425			assert.NotEmpty(t, res.Detail)
2426		})
2427	
2428		t.Run("no changes stays non-success", func(t *testing.T) {
2429			workDir, baseSHA, _ := setupRepo(t)
2430			// Harness does nothing → worktree HEAD == BaseRev → outcome "no-changes".
2431			res := runWithDirective(t, workDir, baseSHA, "no-op\n")
2432			assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, res.Status)
2433			assert.NotEmpty(t, res.Detail)
2434		})
2435	
2436		t.Run("execution failure dominates preserved outcome", func(t *testing.T) {
2437			workDir, baseSHA, _ := setupRepo(t)
2438			// Real commit + non-zero exit: LandBeadResult preserves (commits
2439			// exist) but ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus must still report
2440			// execution_failed because ExitCode != 0 dominates.
2441			res := runWithDirective(t, workDir, baseSHA,
2442				"create-file out.txt content\ncommit feat: add out\nset-exit 1\n")
2443			assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
2444			assert.NotEmpty(t, res.Detail)
2445		})
2446	
2447		t.Run("error outcome stays execution failure", func(t *testing.T) {
2448			workDir, baseSHA, _ := setupRepo(t)
2449			// Pin ExitCode=-1 (canonical timeout shape), then trip a synthetic
2450			// harness failure before any commit is made. LandBeadResult maps
2451			// (ExitCode!=0, no commits) to outcome "error".
2452			res := runWithDirective(t, workDir, baseSHA,
2453				"set-exit -1\nfail-during 1\n")
2454			assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
2455			assert.NotEmpty(t, res.Detail)
2456		})
2457	
2458		t.Run("land conflict", func(t *testing.T) {
2459			workDir, baseSHA, runGit := setupRepo(t)
2460			// Advance main with a sibling edit to out.txt, then have the worker
2461			// commit a conflicting version starting from baseSHA. Real Land()
2462			// hits a merge conflict and preserves with reason "merge conflict".
2463			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "out.txt"), []byte("sibling\n"), 0o644))
2464			runGit("add", "out.txt")
2465			runGit("commit", "-m", "feat: sibling conflicting change")
2466			res := runWithDirective(t, workDir, baseSHA,
2467				"create-file out.txt worker\ncommit feat: worker add out\n")
2468			assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict, res.Status)
2469			assert.NotEmpty(t, res.Detail)
2470		})
2471	}

2571	// TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected verifies that when execute-bead
2572	// invokes the embedded-agent harness, its session/telemetry runtime state is
2573	// redirected into a DDx-owned directory inside the execution bundle instead
2574	// of being written at the worktree root. Regression guard for ddx-cba2dc64.
2575	func TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected(t *testing.T) {
2576		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2577			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111cafe",
2578			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222beef",
2579		}
2580	
2581		// Snapshot of wtPath root contents captured during Run so the assertion
2582		// can run before ExecuteBead removes the worktree on return.
2583		var wtPathDuringRun string
2584		var wtRootBefore []string
2585		var wtRootAfter []string
2586		var sessionLogDirSeen string
2587		simulatedSessionFile := "agent-embedded-session.jsonl"
2588	
2589		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{
2590			result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "agent"},
2591			sideEffect: func(opts agent.RunOptions) error {
2592				wtPathDuringRun = opts.WorkDir
2593				sessionLogDirSeen = opts.SessionLogDir
2594	
2595				// Capture worktree root listing before simulating writes.
2596				entries, err := os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
2597				if err != nil {
2598					return err
2599				}
2600				for _, e := range entries {
2601					wtRootBefore = append(wtRootBefore, e.Name())
2602				}
2603	
2604				// Simulate the embedded-agent harness writing runtime state.
2605				// It MUST land in opts.SessionLogDir, not opts.WorkDir. If the
2606				// execute-bead wiring is broken and SessionLogDir is empty or the
2607				// worktree root, this write will land at the worktree root and
2608				// the post-check below will catch it.
2609				if opts.SessionLogDir == "" {
2610					return fmt.Errorf("embedded agent runner received empty SessionLogDir; runtime state would land at worktree root")
2611				}
2612				if err := os.MkdirAll(opts.SessionLogDir, 0o755); err != nil {
2613					return err
2614				}
2615				if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(opts.SessionLogDir, simulatedSessionFile), []byte(`{"event":"started"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
2616					return err
2617				}
2618	
2619				// Capture worktree root listing after simulated writes.
2620				entries, err = os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
2621				if err != nil {
2622					return err
2623				}
2624				for _, e := range entries {
2625					wtRootAfter = append(wtRootAfter, e.Name())
2626				}
2627				return nil
2628			},
2629		}
2630		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2631	
2632		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead", "--harness", "agent")
2633		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome, "execute-bead should succeed for this test")
2634	
2635		// The runner must have received a SessionLogDir override.
2636		require.NotEmpty(t, sessionLogDirSeen, "execute-bead must pass a SessionLogDir to the embedded harness")
2637	
2638		// The override must point inside the tracked execution bundle, not at
2639		// the worktree root.
2640		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ExecutionDir, "execute-bead must record an execution bundle dir")
2641		bundleAbs := filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, filepath.FromSlash(res.ExecutionDir))
2642		absLog, err := filepath.Abs(sessionLogDirSeen)
2643		require.NoError(t, err)
2644		absBundle, err := filepath.Abs(bundleAbs)
2645		require.NoError(t, err)
2646		assert.Truef(t, strings.HasPrefix(absLog, absBundle+string(filepath.Separator)),
2647			"SessionLogDir (%s) must be inside the execution bundle (%s)", absLog, absBundle)
2648		assert.Equal(t, "embedded", filepath.Base(absLog),
2649			"SessionLogDir must be the bundle's embedded/ subdirectory")
2650	
2651		// The worktree root must not gain any files during the run.
2652		require.NotEmpty(t, wtPathDuringRun)
2653		assert.Equal(t, wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter,
2654			"worktree root entries must not change while the embedded harness runs (before=%v after=%v)",
2655			wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter)
2656		assert.NotContains(t, wtRootAfter, simulatedSessionFile,

2662		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(sessionLogDirSeen, simulatedSessionFile))

22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}

64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()

126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)

132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}

147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}

211	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
212	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
213		return ExecuteBeadReport{
214			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
215			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
216			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
217			Harness:            res.Harness,
218			Model:              res.Model,
219			Status:             res.Status,
220			Detail:             res.Detail,
221			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
222			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
223			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
225			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
226		}
227	}

206	// fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit returns a LandingAdvancer callback that maps the
207	// fake git's Merge/UpdateRef semantics onto the coordinator-pattern advancer
208	// interface. Semantically, "mergeCalls" now means "number of times
209	// LandBeadResult invoked the advancer (attempted to advance the target
210	// branch)". Used by tests that were written against the old Merge() path so
211	// they continue to pass after the land-coordinator refactor.
212	func fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git *fakeExecuteBeadGit) func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
213		return func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
214			if err := git.Merge("", res.ResultRev); err != nil {
215				preserveRef := agent.PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
216				// Record the preserve ref in the fake's refs map so tests that
217				// assert git.refs[preserveRef] continue to pass.
218				_ = git.UpdateRef("", preserveRef, res.ResultRev)
219				return &agent.LandResult{
220					Status:      "preserved",
221					PreserveRef: preserveRef,
222					Reason:      "merge failed",
223				}, nil
224			}
225			return &agent.LandResult{Status: "landed", NewTip: res.ResultRev}, nil
226		}
227	}

292	func copyTree(src, dst string) error {
293		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
294			if err != nil {
295				return err
296			}
297			rel, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
298			if err != nil {
299				return err
300			}
301			target := filepath.Join(dst, rel)
302			if info.IsDir() {
303				return os.MkdirAll(target, info.Mode())
304			}
305			if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0o755); err != nil {
306				return err
307			}
308			in, err := os.Open(path)
309			if err != nil {
310				return err
311			}
312			defer in.Close()
313			out, err := os.Create(target)
314			if err != nil {
315				return err
316			}
317			if _, err := io.Copy(out, in); err != nil {
318				out.Close()
319				return err
320			}
321			if err := out.Close(); err != nil {
322				return err
323			}
324			return os.Chmod(target, info.Mode())
325		})
326	}

352		return os.Chmod(dst, info.Mode())
353	}

355	// TestExecuteBeadMerge verifies that when the agent produces a real commit and
356	// the target branch can advance, the outcome is "merged". Exercises real git
357	// plumbing (worktree add/remove, ref update, file sync) and the script harness
358	// driving an actual commit — no fakes for the components under test.
359	func TestExecuteBeadMerge(t *testing.T) {
360		workDir := t.TempDir()
361	
362		scrubEnv := func() []string {
363			parent := os.Environ()
364			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
365			for _, kv := range parent {
366				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
367					continue
368				}
369				env = append(env, kv)
370			}
371			return env
372		}
373		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
374			t.Helper()
375			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
376			cmd.Dir = workDir
377			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
378			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
379			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
380			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
381		}
382	
383		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
384		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
385		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
386		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
387		runGit("add", ".")
388		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
389	
390		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
391			ID:        "my-bead",
392			Title:     "Test execute-bead merge",
393			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
394			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
395		})
396		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
397		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
398		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
399	
400		// Directive file drives the script harness to make one real commit in the
401		// worker worktree, replacing fakeAgentRunner's canned Result struct.
402		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
403		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
404			"create-file hello.txt hello-content\n"+
405				"commit feat: add hello\n",
406		), 0o644))
407	
408		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
409		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
410		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
411	
412		root := f.NewRootCommand()
413		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
414			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
415		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
416		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
417	
418		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
419		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
420		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
421		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
422		assert.NotEqual(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev)
423		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
424		assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
425		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
426	
427		// Real-git assertion: main must have advanced to ResultRev via Land().
428		assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD"),
429			"main should advance to ResultRev after a successful merge")
430	
431		// The worker worktree must have been created and cleaned up: no entries
432		// under the execute-bead prefix remain in `git worktree list`.
433		wtList := runGit("worktree", "list", "--porcelain")
434		assert.NotContains(t, wtList, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"my-bead-",
435			"execute-bead worktree should be removed after the run")
436	
437		// File from the agent's commit must be materialized on main through real
438		// merge/ff semantics (regression guard for ddx-eaebaffb: update-ref
439		// bypassing the working tree).
440		content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "hello.txt"))
441		require.NoError(t, err, "hello.txt should materialize on main after Land()")
442		assert.Equal(t, "hello-content", string(content))
443	}
